scope: Read-only P6 retrospective review of worker coordination, integration ownership, registry drift, and repair-loop process.

findings.summary: Multi-worker slices completed useful isolated work, but early coordination let worker PASS be interpreted too close to milestone PASS.
A0 serial integration improved late in P6, but the terminal loop ledger still drifted from the final reviewed head.
The repair budget guard stopped further implementation churn after 6 of 6 repairs and forced a retrospective instead of starting P6-R1.

recommendations: Keep formal implementation workers as visible worktree threads only.
Run integration checks after each dependency layer instead of waiting until all workers finish.
Make A0 the only owner of terminal loop state, final review packet inputs, and milestone PASS language.
